Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course teaches methods like 'deep interview' and 'observation and reflection on life', which are applicable to mental health and well-being research (Target 3.4).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The core method of 'critical ethnography' is explicitly designed to study and critique social inequalities, directly aligning with Target 10.2 (social inclusion) and 10.3 (equal opportunity).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course requires 'team fieldwork investigation', fostering collaborative skills. The research methods taught can be used to analyze multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.16, 17.17).
